cast.name: 维克托克拉维霍 Víctor Clavijo

writer.name: Manuel H. Martín Manuel H. Martín

director.name: Manuel H. Martín Manuel H. Martín

introduction: Bella was only seven years old when her mother abandoned the family. From a young age, she was forced to take care of the household, her studies, and her father, José, who suffered from depression. At eighteen, Bella meets Ponce, a 22-year-old graffiti artist with aspirations of becoming a painter. She sees him as her “Prince Charming,” a romantic, dreamy, and idealistic soul—just like her. Their love blossoms quickly, and shortly after meeting, they get married. Ponce becomes her entire world, and Bella gradually distances herself from her father, her childhood friends Mauro and Patricia, and even from her studies and ambitions. Six years later, Ponce and Bella have achieved great prosperity. Thanks to their art gallery, they have two children and live in a luxurious mansion, appearing to be a successful and happy family. However, since the beginning of their marriage, Bella has been a victim of abuse. What started as subtle manipulation escalated into monstrous nightmares and violent aggression. One dark night, Bella finally awakens from the nightmare she’s been living and gathers all her strength to escape the hell—and the monster—that Ponce has become.
